summary: Descriptive overview of thirdpartybeneficiaries in substantive law: intended vs incidental, vesting rights.
key_points: intended vs incidental is a fundamental concept in contract law.
Legal standards for thirdpartybeneficiaries are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine thirdpartybeneficiaries in litigation.
The doctrine of thirdpartybeneficiaries has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding thirdpartybeneficiaries requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
